title: 2 Samuel: Kingdom and Covenant
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of 2 samuel: kingdom and covenant and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study 2 samuel: kingdom and covenant and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: What did God promise David in the Davidic covenant of 2 Samuel 7? Answer: that David's son would build the temple, God would be a father to him, and David's throne and kingdom would be established forever.
segments.correct_answer: David's throne and kingdom would be established forever, and God would be father to his son
acceptable_variants: an everlasting dynasty — God would be father to david's son and establish his throne forever
the davidic covenant — eternal throne and father-son relationship with david's line
2 Samuel 7 — god promises david an eternal dynasty

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: God's unconditional promise to David in 2 Samuel 7 that his dynasty would be established forever and God would be father to his son; the foundation for the messianic expectation of a coming king from David's line
The prophet who delivered God's covenant promise to David in 2 Samuel 7 and later confronted David after the sin with Bathsheba
